title: fix: stop nanobanana from clobbering reference images
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
## Problem
nanobanana models (esp. nanobanana-2) ignore user reference images at non-square sizes — output drifts from the supplied images.

## Root cause
`processNanobananaRequest` applied a legacy transparent-image aspect-ratio hack (from #4162, before Vertex exposed native aspect control) to **all** nanobanana models on non-1:1 requests:
- appended a blank transparent image as the **last** reference image, and
- appended a post-prompt: *"…completely replacing the last image while keeping its dimensions. Make sure no blank areas are left."*

With user reference images present, the request became `[userRefImage, transparentImage]` + a "replace the last image" instruction — diluting/overriding reference adherence.

## Why it's safe to remove
`imageConfig.aspectRatio` is now set **unconditionally for all nanobanana models** (`vertexAIClient.ts`), so the hack is redundant. Verified against prod `nanobanana` (gemini-2.5-flash-image) **without** the hack:
- 16:9 (1280×720) → 1344×768 ✓
- 9:16 (720×1280) → 768×1344 ✓
- 21:9 (2520×1080) → 1536×672 ✓ (a ratio the transparent-canvas trick can't produce — proves native config drives it)

## Changes
- Remove `processNanobananaRequest` + the transparent-image append
- Delete now-unused `utils/transparentImage.ts`
- Reference images pass through untouched; aspect ratio handled natively

**−199 / +16 lines.** tsc clean, biome clean, all 108 gen image tests pass.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Nanobanana remembers
gist.blurb: We cleared out some old aspect-ratio cobwebs that were confusing the nanobanana models. Your reference images will now be respected perfectly, even at non-square sizes.
gist.summary: Removed a legacy transparent-image hack from the nanobanana pipeline, fixing an issue where user-provided reference images were ignored at non-square aspect ratios.
gist.impact: Reference images now work correctly with nanobanana models across all aspect ratios, as dimensions are now handled natively by Vertex AI.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee holding up a reference photo (polaroid) to the Monitor Robot, who is successfully painting an exact match on a wide, non-square canvas.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot holding up a small polaroid reference photo to a chunky robot with a CRT monitor head. The robot is happily painting an exact match of the photo onto a wide rectangular canvas. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
